To determine whether these magnets attract or repel each other, we need to consider their magnetic poles. Magnetic poles are either N (north) or S (south). Magnets with the same pole will repel each other, while magnets with opposite poles will attract each other.

Let's analyze the poles:

1. The first magnet shows both an S pole and an N pole.
2. The second magnet shows an N pole and an S pole.

These are opposite poles. When magnets with opposite poles are placed close to each other, they will attract.

Final Answer: \boxed{attract}<|im_end|><|endoftext|><|endoftext|><|endoftext|><|endoftext|><|endoftext|><|endoftext|><|endoftext|>
